title: Weekend 2 — Shotfuse Personality (DoD milestone)
description:
Milestone tracking for Weekend 2 Definition of Done per SPEC.md §2. Ships when 10 consecutive captures satisfy: ≤ 2 predicted≠chosen, ≤ 2 chooser-appearances; annotations round-trip byte-identical (same machine + minor) / SSIM ≥ 0.995 across 26.x patchlevels.

Depends on: P4.2 (Router), P5.1 (annotations), P5.2 (window/fullscreen), P6.2 (SCA), P6.3 (witness), P7.2 (DoD harness).
Spec: §2 Weekend 2 DoD verbatim. If this test fails, stop adding features and fix the Router.

title: Weekend 1 — Shotfuse Spine (DoD milestone)
description:
Milestone tracking for Weekend 1 Definition of Done per SPEC.md §2. Ships when hotkey → drag → release produces clipboard PNG + .shot package within p95 ≤ 1.5s warm / ≤ 3.0s cold, Cmd+Shift+G returns <100ms, fuse-gc deletes expired unpinned.

Depends on: P1.3 (capture finalization), P2.2 (search overlay), P3.2 (launch agent), P6.1 (CLI), P7.1 (DoD harness).
Spec: §2 Weekend 1 DoD verbatim.

title: W7/P7.2: Weekend 2 DoD harness (Router + annotations)
description:
10-consecutive-captures fixture + synthetic telemetry.jsonl. Asserts (a) ≤ 2 predicted≠chosen; (b) ≤ 2 chooser-appearances (top≤0.85 OR second≥0.4). Annotations byte-identical round-trip same machine/same minor; SSIM ≥ 0.995 across synthetic patchlevels.

Spec: §2 Weekend 2 DoD verbatim.
Test: This IS the test. Tests/DoD/WeekendTwoDoDTests.swift.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P7.2

title: W7/P7.1: Weekend 1 DoD harness (observable test)
description:
End-to-end Swift Testing: press-hotkey → drag → release. Asserts p95 ≤ 1.5s warm / ≤ 3.0s cold / p50 ≤ 600ms warm (hotkey-release → clipboard-write AND .shot fsync returned). Verifies .shot has master.png + ocr.json + context.json + thumb.jpg + manifest.json with all required fields. Cmd+Shift+G <100ms. fuse-gc deletes expired unpinned.

Spec: §2 Weekend 1 DoD verbatim.
Test: This IS the test. Tests/DoD/WeekendOneDoDTests.swift.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P7.1

title: W7/P7.4: Observability + redaction audit
description:
Static check (Swift Testing AST assertion) that no log statement passes raw ocr.text, raw context.clipboard, or raw file paths beyond sha256[:8]. Telemetry validator asserts exactly 6-field shape {id, ts, predicted, chosen, top_score, second_score}.

Spec: §13.5 telemetry; §17.2 redaction.
Test: ObservabilityAuditTests — (a) build fails if banned field is logged directly; (b) telemetry validator rejects extra fields.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P7.4

title: W6/P6.2: SensitiveContentAnalysis + Redact-and-re-save
description:
Post-capture SCSensitivityAnalyzer on master.png. manifest.sensitivity ∈ {["nudity"], ["password_field"], ["card_number"], ["none"], or combinations}. Limbo "Redact and re-save" creates a NEW .shot with blurred regions; original master.png byte-unchanged.

Spec: §5 I3 (master immutable); §13.4 SCA contract + Invariant 3 guarantee.
Test: SCATests — (a) password-field fixture ⇒ ["password_field"]; (b) redaction creates new .shot with blurs; (c) original master byte-identical post-redaction.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P6.2

title: W6/P6.1: CLI surface (shot last|list|show|system status|fuse-gc|export|uninstall)
description:
CLI/ target. Exactly the commands in §16 — nothing else. shot system status surfaces TCC gates + library count + fuse-gc last-run + launch-agent status.

Spec: §16 CLI surface (exact set).
Test: CLITests — (a) shot last --ocr/--path/--copy per spec; (b) shot system export tarball omits telemetry.jsonl; (c) shot system uninstall removes launch agent, preserves library.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P6.1

title: W4/P4.2: Router predictor + decision rule + telemetry
description:
Core/Router/Router.swift actor. Three predictors (§7 taxonomy). Auto-deliver if top>0.85 AND second<0.4, else 3-option chooser; 3s toast; Cmd+Z opens chooser. Append {id, ts, predicted, chosen, top_score, second_score} to telemetry.jsonl — no other fields.

Spec: §4 Router vocab; §7 taxonomy; §7.1 decision rule; §7.3 side-effects (create screenshots/, never touch .gitignore/git, Obsidian-fail → clipboard + log); §13.5 telemetry schema; rotate at 10 MB keep 2.
Test: RouterTests — (a) Xcode+git-root ⇒ ~/Projects/<root>/screenshots/ top>0.85; (b) Obsidian-frontmost ⇒ obsidian://daily; (c) unwritable target ⇒ clipboard + unified-log; (d) telemetry line exactly 6 fields, no OCR/clipboard/path leak.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P4.2

title: W5/P5.1: Annotation canvas (AppKit NSView) + annotations.json
description:
AppKit NSView canvas; tools: arrow/text/blur_rect. annotations.json per §6.4 (vector model in master-pixel space). Re-render from master.png + annotations.json is byte-identical on same machine/same macOS minor, SSIM ≥ 0.995 across macOS 26.x patchlevels.

Spec: §5 I2 (AppKit), I3 (re-renders from master; NEVER modifies master), I6 (master-pixel coords); §6.4 defaults (#FF3B30 arrow, 4pt, system body font, sigma 12pt).
Test: AnnotationsTests — (a) byte-identical re-render on fixture; (b) SSIM ≥ 0.995 across two fixture render paths; (c) coords in master-pixel at render.
Full test contract: .claude/session-plan.md P5.1

title: W2/P2.2: Cmd+Shift+G search overlay
description:
AppKit floating panel. RegisterEventHotKey (Carbon) — no global event taps. Live FTS5 query. Arrow-key nav. Enter opens show-sheet.

Test: SearchOverlayTests — (a) p95 query <100ms on 1k-row fixture; (b) hotkey-fail logs + badges menubar; (c) FTS top-1 correct for OCR text, window title, bundle ID.

title: W1/P1.3: Capture-pipeline wiring + AX-sync enrichment (finalizing)

capturing→finalizing writes master.png + thumb.jpg (256px), syncs AX snapshot (kAXDocumentAttribute/kAXURLAttribute), resolves git-root, applies SENSITIVE_BUNDLES gate, writes context.json + manifest.json (UUIDv7, expires_at=now+24h, pinned=false, mode="verbal").

Spec: §5 I1, I3, I12; §6.1 manifest; §6.2 context + clipboard gate; §13.3 SENSITIVE_BUNDLES; Spike A (hq-91t) → sync AX is fine.
Test: CaptureFinalizationTests — (a) 1Password-frontmost aborts to failed, no .shot written; (b) clipboard skipped when last-modifier ∈ SENSITIVE_BUNDLES within 60s; (c) ax_available=false when AX-denied; (d) manifest fields exact.

Structural foundation. Creates Shotfuse.xcworkspace at repo root containing App/ (SwiftUI menubar), CLI/ (shot binary), Core/ (local SwiftPM). Info.plist has LSUIElement=true, bundle dev.friquelme.shotfuse, TCC usage strings for Screen Recording + Accessibility.

title: Spike B: Spotlight + QuickLook on .shot UTI packages
description: Determines whether Shotfuse can lean on Spotlight as a nice-to-have on top of its SQLite+FTS5 index (SPEC.md §5 Invariant 9), and whether a custom QuickLook generator plugin is needed for v0.1. Outcome does NOT gate Weekend 1 — SQLite+FTS5 is the load-bearing index regardless — but a negative result commits Shotfuse to shipping a QuickLook plugin later.
design: UTImported declarations in Info.plist. Use mdimport and mdfind from Terminal to verify Spotlight indexing. Boot state: mdutil -E / between runs to force fresh indexing. QuickLook verified via qlmanage -p.
acceptance_criteria: Minimal Xcode project registers .shot as a package UTI (conforms to com.apple.package + public.composite-content). Writes a synthetic .shot containing fake manifest.json, ocr.json (with unique test string), master.png, thumb.jpg. Verify (a) Spotlight returns the package when searching for the unique OCR test string; (b) QuickLook preview shows thumb.jpg when the .shot is selected in Finder. Record verdict {spotlight: yes|no, quicklook: yes|no|needs-plugin} in the issue. Test project committed under spikes/uti-spotlight/.
notes:
Executed 2026-04-21 on macOS 26.0.1.

SPOTLIGHT: NO.
- UTI registered cleanly via lsregister -f. `lsregister -dump` shows:
    type id: dev.friquelme.shotfuse.shot (0x13c70)
    conforms to: com.apple.package, public.composite-content
    flags: active exported untrusted
    tags: .shot
- Finder shows `.shot` as "Shotfuse Capture Package" (kMDItemKind) — free UX win.
- BUT: `mdimport -t -d1` reports: "Imported ... of type 'dev.friquelme.shotfuse.shot'
  with no plugIn. 26 attributes returned". Only default filesystem attributes indexed.
- `mdfind "SHOTFUSE-SPIKE-OCR-63E7421B-88DD-4DEE-A824-DDB3CF2C70DB"` returns empty,
  both whole-volume and scoped via -onlyin.
- `mdls -name kMDItemTextContent` on the package's ocr.json returns null — Spotlight
  does not descend into package directories by design.
- Package opacity (the thing that makes .shot appear as one bundle in Finder) is
  EXACTLY what blocks Spotlight from indexing inner ocr.json. These goals conflict.
- Mitigation for later: Core Spotlight (CSSearchableItem API) can push capture
  metadata from Shotfuse's own library scanner to Spotlight, separately from the
  package UTI. That's post-v0.1 work.

QUICKLOOK: NEEDS-PLUGIN.
- `qlmanage -m plugins` confirms the system ships Package.qlgenerator registered
  for `com.apple.package`. Theory: QL should walk the conformance tree and use
  it for our .shot. Practice: it doesn't render useful content.
- Finder QuickLook (spacebar on selected .shot) shows: generic document icon
  with "?" overlay + "SHOT" extension label + 61 KB + mtime. No thumb.jpg,
  no manifest preview.
- `qlmanage -t` hangs indefinitely from a non-GUI shell (~60s killed) — expected
  quicklookd sandboxing quirk, not the real signal. The real signal is the
  Finder visual above.
- For v0.1 this is ACCEPTABLE (pessimistic hypothesis confirmed). A ~100-line
  QLPreviewProvider subclass (QuickLookThumbnailing framework, modern Swift
  alternative to the old .qlgenerator C API) can render thumb.jpg — roadmap
  item, not a blocker.

Unique OCR marker used this run: SHOTFUSE-SPIKE-OCR-63E7421B-88DD-4DEE-A824-DDB3CF2C70DB

Impact on SPEC:
- Invariant 9 (SQLite+FTS5 as load-bearing index) stands and is vindicated.
- §6 thumb.jpg annotation "preview for pinboard + QuickLook" is aspirational;
  deferring the QuickLook plugin is non-breaking.
- §3 non-goals / §11 deferred list: add "QuickLook preview plugin for .shot"
  with revisit trigger "after v0.1 daily use confirms the generic icon is
  annoying enough to warrant the plugin".
close_reason: spotlight: no (requires CSSearchableItem integration; v0.1 stays on SQLite+FTS5 per Invariant 9). quicklook: needs-plugin (generic '?' + SHOT extension icon; QLPreviewProvider subclass on roadmap, non-blocking for v0.1).

title: Spike A: AX tree extraction latency benchmark
description: Determines whether context.json enrichment can run sync at capture time or must be deferred to post-capture async. Blocks Weekend 1 CaptureEngine state-machine implementation — specifically the capturing→finalizing transition. If p95 > 50ms, SPEC.md §5 Invariant 1 gains an 'AX enrichment is async' clause and the Limbo HUD must render without context on first paint.
design: Use AXUIElementCreateApplication + AXUIElementCopyAttributeValue for kAXFocusedWindowAttribute and kAXFocusedUIElementAttribute. Run on AC power, screen unlocked, target apps already launched and frontmost at least once before the measurement run. Measure wall-clock using ContinuousClock.
acceptance_criteria: ~30-line Swift benchmark extracts { bundle_id, window_title, focused file_url or browser_url } via AXUIElement APIs. Run 20 iterations each against Xcode, VS Code, Safari, Slack, Obsidian (5 apps × 20 = 100 samples). Report p50 / p95 / p99. Decision recorded in the issue: sync if p95 ≤ 50ms, else async with post-capture enrichment. Benchmark code committed under spikes/ax-latency/.
notes:
Executed 2026-04-21 on macOS 26.0.1, battery power (not AC — per-run spread ruled out downclock as
a factor given the huge margin), Warp terminal with AX permission. 4 apps × 20 iters = 80 samples.
VS Code not installed; skipped (noted, does not change verdict).

Three consecutive runs, aggregate p95 / p99:
  run 1 (cold):  p95=11.45ms  p99=1511.67ms  — single Xcode iteration spiked due to activation settle
  run 2:         p95= 0.34ms  p99=  22.50ms
  run 3:         p95= 0.47ms  p99=  21.97ms

Steady-state p50 across runs: 0.08–0.32ms per app. Median run p95 = 0.47ms, ~107× below the 50ms
gate. Run 1's 1.5s outlier was activation settling, not the AX call itself — at actual capture time
the frontmost app is already active when the hotkey fires, so that path never executes.

Per-app, focused-URL extraction verified:
  Xcode:    file:///Users/.../settings.json  (kAXDocumentAttribute on focused window)
  Safari:   https://svo-test.de/             (kAXURLAttribute on focused window)
  Slack:    <nil> — no URL surface via AX (expected; §6.2 marks file_url/browser_url optional)
  Obsidian: <nil> — same

DECISION: sync AX enrichment at capture time. SPEC.md §5 Invariant 1 remains "enrichment during
capturing→finalizing transition" with no async-clause addition. Limbo HUD renders with context on
first paint.

Raw output from all three runs captured in commit 1aa8f36 README + this note.
close_reason: sync AX enrichment viable (median run p95=0.47ms, worst p95=11.45ms; both far below 50ms gate)

title: Adversarial audit of SPEC.md v0.1
description: Run octo:spec-style completeness challenge against SPEC.md. Surface missing requirements, contradictions, edge cases. Produce scored audit + proposed delta in SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md. Non-destructive — do not modify SPEC.md.
acceptance_criteria: SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md exists with dimensional scoring, severity-ranked findings, and concrete delta proposals. SPEC.md unchanged.
close_reason: SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md written. 53/100 score, 4 P0 blockers, 14 P1/P2/P3, paste-ready deltas for all non-P3 findings.

value: When doing contract-first parallel execution, the orchestrator's preflight commit can race against isolation:worktree spawn — workers may start from the pre-preflight HEAD and reinvent the contract types themselves. Mitigation options: (1) push the preflight commit before spawning, or (2) include a first-step in the worker prompt: 'git rev-parse HEAD must show <preflight-sha>; if not, git checkout -b feat/<id> <preflight-sha>'. Observed 2026-04-21 during W1 spine: worker A (hq-nqi) started at de3c51f and reinvented RegionSelection/DisplayMetadata in Core/Sources/Core/Overlay/RegionGeometry.swift; B and C correctly started at 6107919 and consumed the preflight types. Resolution: adopted worker A's types as canonical (closer to IOKit ground truth — UInt32 vendor/product/serial, plus globalFrame for hit-test), deleted the preflight file, patched B/C fixtures. Net result: one extra merge-resolution commit, still faster than serial.

Product name: Shotfuse (decided 2026-04-21 via multi-AI naming brainstorm). App display name Shotfuse, CLI binary shot, package extension .shot. Pattern follows GitHub/gh and Homebrew/brew: brand name for README/marketing, short verb for daily use. GitHub user 'shotfuse' is squatted by inactive account — publish as github.com/<owner>/shotfuse or use an org like shotfuse-app if needed. shotfuse.com domain available. No Homebrew or App Store collision.

Spike A closed 2026-04-21 (hq-91t). AX tree extraction on macOS 26 is effectively free — median-run aggregate p95 = 0.47ms across Xcode/Safari/Slack/Obsidian × 20 iters, three runs. Decision: sync AX enrichment at capture time. SPEC.md §5 Invariant 1 keeps the capturing→finalizing transition as the enrichment point (no async clause). Limbo HUD paints with context on first frame. Verified focused-URL extraction: kAXDocumentAttribute on Xcode, kAXURLAttribute on Safari. Slack/Obsidian don't expose URLs via AX — matches §6.2 optionality. Spike code: spikes/ax-latency/. Benchmark reusable for regression if we ever suspect the conclusion broke.

Rejected names (do not re-suggest): Ferry, Relay, Mote, Trail, Ephemera, Witness (too metaphorical — did not read as capture tool at a glance). Shutter (GitHub collision with active Linux screenshot tool shutter-project/shutter, 633 stars, maintained). Lightbox (jQuery Lightbox owns SEO). Frame (too generic to rank). Prism (prism.js owns namespace). Runner-ups seriously considered in final round: Dailies (film-production vocab, 24h fuse thesis match) and Plate (master+derived package format match). Chose Shotfuse for legibility — 'shot' reads instantly as capture, 'fuse' carries expiry. Full brainstorm artifacts: BRAINSTORM.md in repo root plus multi-AI round outputs in /tmp tasks/ dir.

Shotfuse v0.1 execution plan filed 2026-04-21. 23 beads issues (21 phase tasks + 2 DoD milestones: hq-ma3 W1, hq-ni3 W2). Critical path: P0.1 hq-cx5 (Xcode workspace) is the only ready issue — everything else blocked. Full plan with test contracts at .claude/session-plan.md, intent at .claude/session-intent.md. Execution deferred: user will run /octo:embrace "Shotfuse v0.1 (W1 + W2)" when ready.

Spike B closed 2026-04-21 (hq-alo). UTI registration works cleanly: Finder shows .shot as 'Shotfuse Capture Package' out of the box. Spotlight: NO — mdimport says 'Imported with no plugIn. 26 attributes returned'; package opacity (com.apple.package conformance) is precisely what blocks indexing of inner ocr.json, and these goals are fundamentally incompatible. Core Spotlight CSSearchableItem API is the post-v0.1 path — Shotfuse's library scanner pushes metadata to Spotlight separately from the package UTI. QuickLook: NEEDS-PLUGIN — Finder spacebar shows generic '?' doc icon with SHOT extension label, no thumb.jpg rendering, despite com.apple.package / public.composite-content conformance. A ~100-line QLPreviewProvider subclass (modern QuickLookThumbnailing framework, not the old .qlgenerator C API) can render thumb.jpg. Non-blocking for v0.1 — SPEC Invariant 9 (SQLite+FTS5 is the load-bearing index) stands. Spike code: spikes/uti-spotlight/.

Do NOT run 'git pull --rebase' after doing local --no-ff merges of feature branches when origin is strictly behind. Rebase will unravel the merge commits and try to replay each feature branch's commits linearly on top of origin, hitting the exact same conflicts you already resolved in the merge commits. Symptom observed 2026-04-21: rebase step 3/5 reintroduced the DisplayMetadata type-mismatch conflict from the hq-nqi+hq-cvw merge. Fix: 'git rebase --abort' then just 'git push' (origin is behind, FF push works). The CLAUDE.md session-close protocol says 'git pull --rebase' as a sync step — only run it when origin has moved ahead, not unconditionally after merges.

Parallel execution pattern that worked 2026-04-21: spawn Agent with isolation: worktree + general-purpose subagent per independent beads issue. Each worker: (1) claims via bd update --claim BEFORE coding, (2) implements + tests + commits on its own worktree branch, (3) reports BRANCH+SHA+TESTS. Orchestrator merges --no-ff sequentially, then runs bd close centrally (workers' staged .beads edits don't reliably make it into their commit unless you explicitly tell them to git add .beads/issues.jsonl). Conflicts on .beads/issues.jsonl during merge are trivial — resolve with --ours then rerun bd close to make state authoritative. Cleanup: git worktree remove -f -f <path> to override harness locks after agent returns. Three P0.x tasks on Shotfuse completed this way in ~2 min wall time vs serial.

Platform target: macOS 26+ (raised floor from brainstorm's original 14+ on 2026-04-21). This unlocks native SCRecordingOutput, captureMicrophone, showMouseClicks, includeChildWindows, SensitiveContentAnalysis — no re-implementation of click rings. Stack: Swift + SwiftUI (settings/inspector) + AppKit (capture overlays, Limbo HUD, annotation canvas where focus/coordinate determinism matters). Test framework: Swift Testing. Single CaptureEngine actor owns state machine; SwiftUI views never own SCStream.

Repo layout: Xcode workspace at repo root with split targets — App/ (menu-bar UI), CLI/ (shot binary), and Core/ as a local Swift Package for capture engine, package format, indexing. Willingness to invest upfront for long-term maintainability confirmed by user. Distribution: open source, public GitHub, free for everyone. No commercial or subscription pressure.
